post_extraction_enhancement.notes: broadcast_strong is preferred because it preserves more voice richness/body than gate/denoise variants while still improving strength and presence with less saturation than speech_level.

evidence.note: v1 had clean voice extraction with music removed; v2 close; MDX23C preserves voice body but less clean here.
v1 removed side tech noises and reduced siren more than other candidates, though strong siren tone remains because it overlaps vocal-like bands.
ClearVoice SE had no noticeable benefit; SR variants added artificial pitch/brightness and artifacts, so AI super-resolution is rejected for now.
broadcast_strong is the current enhancement lead over speech_level because it keeps good voice power with slightly less saturation. Tiny between-word residue remains visible in Audacity.
Initial soft gate/FFT/wavelet floor cleanup did not visibly reduce the between-word residue. Next test moves the gate before speech leveling and uses a stronger threshold ladder.
gate_pre_level_high is a good candidate: it reduces visible residue better than lower thresholds without being clearly over-aggressive. Further bracketing around this threshold is needed.
Bracketing around gate_pre_level_high did not visibly improve the waveform floor. Switch to compand/downward-expander tests before deciding whether to stop chasing visual flatness.
Compand/downward-expander tests were less good than gate_pre_level_high_denoise; do not pursue expander floor cleanup unless later full-episode evidence changes this.
gate_pre_level_high_denoise flattened some silence sections but removed voice richness. Prefer broadcast_strong to preserve maximum old Spanish voice information.

notes: Current lead for old spa1 voice extraction is MelBand RoFormer InstVoc Duality v1. Current post-extraction enhancement lead is broadcast_strong because it preserves more voice richness/body than stronger cleanup variants. Siren-like effects are tracked as diagnostics but not the main target; final 5.1 bed should retain matching effects from destination audio.
